title: lavatera cretica var. silvestris
intro: Lavatera cretica var. silvestris, also known as the shrubby hollyhock, is a subspecies of the Lavatera cretica plant, a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lavatera cretica var. silvestris include its ability to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and ointments, often used to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
